description: Schedule Inline Editing - Make schedule boxes the entry point for editing instead of header buttons

userStories.title: Inline schedule editing for Burn Buddies
userStories.description: As a user on the Burn Buddy detail page, I can manage my workout schedule entirely through the schedule box area — no header button needed. If no schedule exists, a dashed placeholder invites me to add one. If a schedule exists, tapping it opens the inline editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove the 'Edit Schedule' button from the Burn Buddy detail page header (the toggle button in the top-right area near the back button)

When no workoutSchedule is set, display a dashed-border placeholder box with a '+' icon and 'Add a schedule' text in the schedule display area. Use muted/secondary styling (not the orange of an active schedule).
The placeholder box includes a small chevron (▾) indicator on the right side to hint at interactivity
Clicking the placeholder expands the existing inline day/time editor (day toggle buttons + time input + save button) directly below the placeholder
When a workoutSchedule IS set, the existing orange schedule info box gets cursor-pointer, a subtle hover effect, and a small chevron indicator (▾ when collapsed, ▴ when expanded) on the right side
Clicking the orange schedule box toggles the inline day/time editor open/closed below it, pre-populated with the current schedule days and time
The inline editor includes both 'Save Schedule' and 'Cancel' buttons side-by-side — clicking outside the editor does NOT collapse it
Saving collapses the editor and updates the displayed schedule. Cancel collapses without saving.
The 'Add to Calendar' button remains visible and functional when the editor is open
No regression in other header elements (back button, buddy name)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Inline schedule editing for Burn Squads
userStories.description: As a squad admin on the Burn Squad detail page, I can manage the workout schedule through the schedule box area. Non-admins see the schedule but cannot edit. The existing settings panel schedule editing continues to work independently.
acceptanceCriteria: When no workoutSchedule is set on a squad, display a dashed-border placeholder box with a '+' icon and 'Add a schedule' text in the schedule display area (same style as the Burn Buddy placeholder)
The placeholder includes a small chevron (▾) indicator on the right side
Only squad admins can interact with the placeholder — non-admins see nothing in the schedule area when no schedule exists
Clicking the placeholder (admin only) expands the inline day/time editor below it
When a workoutSchedule IS set, the schedule info box gets cursor-pointer, hover effect, and chevron indicator (▾/▴) — but only for admins. Non-admins see the schedule box without clickable styling.
Clicking the schedule box (admin only) toggles the inline day/time editor open/closed below it, pre-populated with the current schedule
The inline editor includes both 'Save' and 'Cancel' buttons — clicking outside does NOT collapse it
Saving collapses the editor and updates the displayed schedule. Cancel collapses without saving.
The 'Add to Calendar' button remains visible and functional when the editor is open
Schedule editing in the existing settings panel (Edit Settings button) continues to work exactly as before — this is a separate, independent entry point
A separate isEditingSchedule state is used (independent from the existing editing state that controls the settings panel)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The page is ~452 lines. Admin check at line 143 (isAdmin). Schedule display at lines 356-364 uses violet colors. The settings panel edit form is at lines 256-329 and must NOT be changed. Add a new isEditingSchedule boolean state separate from the existing editing state. Follow the same pattern established in US-001 for the buddy page.
